title: cayratia yoshimurae
intro: Cayratia yoshimurae, also known as the Japanese grape or woodbine, is a type of climbing plant native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Cayratia yoshimurae is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being dried and used in capsule or powder form.
